_provenance.machine: node-macos-a / Apple M3 Pro (12 core, arm64) / 36GB unified / macOS 26.5
_provenance.regime: real-model (qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct Q8_0) live arm, UNCONTENDED box, reps=2 (min-over-reps)
_provenance.what_is_new: First clean, uncontended, real-1.5B, all-four-workloads radix live measurement on the M3 Pro. The committed RADIXATTENTION-RESULTS.md real-model live column was SmolLM2-135M on a HEAVILY CONTENDED box (its absolute ms were ~140x inflated, kept only for the within-run ratio). Hit rates / token-speedups / scheduling-theorem here are MODEL-INDEPENDENT and reproduce the committed numbers EXACTLY (88.2/79.5/77.2/86.7%; agents FCFS 62.1%->cache-aware 86.7% = 100% of optimal); only the live wall-clock ratios are the new datum.

axis: cache hit rate (hardware/model-independent) — the metric SGLang's paper headlines; fak runs the same radix-tree + longest-prefix + LRU-leaf algorithm, so on the same workload it reaches the same reuse
engine: fak radixbench — RadixAttention-style prefix cache (internal/radixkv) over the kernel-owned KV cache
headline: cache hit rate (SGLang's axis) + radix_reuse_over_declare_one — radix's automatic cross-subtree discovery vs a WARM single-declared-prefix cache (the serving-realistic baseline). prefill_token_speedup is vs the COLD no-cache baseline — a worst-case REFERENCE only, not a serving baseline anyone ships.
notes: baseline=full prefill per request (COLD no-cache — worst-case reference, not a serving baseline); declare-one-prefix=fak's pre-radix single-declared-prefix reuse (the WARM serving-realistic baseline); radix=internal/radixkv automatic longest-prefix discovery (incl. mid-run split). Live arm is a real kernel prefill per request, bit-identical to recompute (proven in internal/radixkv). Cache-aware order = lexicographic (== DFS == longest-shared-prefix-first), reproducing the paper's optimal-hit-rate-at-budget>=maxlen result.

policy_eviction.note: verdict-driven span eviction (radixkv.EvictNode); shared system prefix + benign sibling preserved. LRU radix caches evict only under memory pressure.

workloads.name: few-shot
workloads.desc: N questions share one few-shot preamble (MMLU 5-shot shape)
workloads.sglang_published: single-level prefix reuse; hit rate within 50-99% band

workloads.name: multi-turn-chat
workloads.desc: T-turn conversation; each turn reuses the full history
workloads.sglang_published: history reuse; large speedup for short outputs (Sec 6.2)

workloads.name: tree-of-thought
workloads.desc: branching reasoning tree; siblings share every ancestor (ToT/GSM-8K shape)

workloads.name: agents
workloads.desc: C concurrent agents share a system prefix; each has a private growing context, arrivals interleaved (ReAct shape)
workloads.sglang_published: two-level reuse (shared system + per-agent chain); cache-aware scheduling recovers hit rate under interleaving
